title: A systematic framework to reduce urban embodied carbon emissions using urban scale simulation

meta_data.Data_Name: National Structure Inventory (NSI) for Chicago metropolitan area
meta_data.Data_summary: A national building-structure inventory compiled by the US Army Corps of Engineers and integrated with FEMA, providing per-building attributes such as structural type, foundation type, floor area, number of floors, and building coordinates. In this study it is the primary source used to assemble the Chicago building stock baseline and to support archetype assignment and scaling of life cycle impacts to individual buildings.
meta_data.Category: Building footprints and land use maps
meta_data.Other_Information: Evidence: Various sources were used to create a comprehensive database with the National Structure Inventory NSI dataset serving as the primary source58, Location: Methods Building stock dataset creation, Confidence: high
meta_data.Geographic_Coverage: United States > Illinois > Chicago metropolitan area

meta_data.Data_Name: Cook County Open Data property characteristics dataset used for envelope and systems
meta_data.Data_summary: A county open-data property dataset providing building-level characteristics including exterior wall and roof materials and also building systems fields such as heating and air conditioning, plus building area and geocoordinates. In this study it is joined to NSI using geocoordinate matching to add envelope and system attributes needed to define archetypes and map life cycle impacts across over one million buildings.
meta_data.Category: Building footprints and land use maps
meta_data.Other_Information: Evidence: To gather the building envelope data the Cook County Open Data was utilized which included information on building wall and roof materials the heating and air conditioning systems building area and geocoordinates, Location: Methods Building stock dataset creation, Confidence: high

meta_data.Data_Name: Integrated Chicago building stock dataset created in this study
meta_data.Data_summary: An author-constructed, building-level integrated dataset for the Chicago metropolitan area created by merging NSI and Cook County Open Data using geocoordinates. It contains over one million buildings with attributes used for archetyping such as structure type, foundation type, exterior wall material, roof type, floor area, number of floors, construction year, and location coordinates. It serves as the baseline inventory for the simulation and for assigning archetypes to specific buildings.
meta_data.Category: Building footprints and land use maps
meta_data.Other_Information: Evidence: By matching the geocoordinates from the Cook County data with those in NSI dataset information for over one million buildings in the Chicago metropolitan area was compiled, Location: Methods Building stock dataset creation, Confidence: high
meta_data.Geographic_Coverage: United States > Illinois > Chicago metropolitan area

meta_data.Data_Name: Athena Impact Estimator modeled archetype LCA outputs dataset
meta_data.Data_summary: A dataset generated by the authors by modeling each building archetype in Athena Impact Estimator v5.5, producing per-archetype life cycle environmental indicators including total global warming potential and other life cycle energy consumption indicators across cradle-to-grave stages. In this study these per-archetype results are scaled and applied to individual buildings in the Chicago inventory to estimate embodied environmental impacts at building and city scale.
meta_data.Other_Information: Evidence: all the archetypes were modeled in Athena Impact Estimator version 5.5 generating a dataset that includes three Life Cycle Energy Consumption indicators especially the total GWP in different lifecycle phases, Location: Methods Archetypes cradle-to-grave life cycle assessment, Confidence: high

meta_data.Data_Name: Final Chicago building-level environmental impact dataset produced in this study
meta_data.Data_summary: An author-generated building-level dataset for the Chicago metropolitan area that augments the integrated building stock inventory with computed environmental impact metrics. Each building record includes assigned archetype code and scaled life cycle impact values such as GWP and other emissions indicators, along with building attributes like year built, floor area, and number of stories. This dataset is used as the direct input to the Monte Carlo style urban policy simulation and the descriptive statistics reported in the results.
meta_data.Other_Information: Evidence: the final dataset was created including over one million buildings with all their environmental impact information year built area and archetype code etc, Location: Methods Archetypes cradle-to-grave life cycle assessment, Confidence: high
meta_data.Geographic_Coverage: United States > Illinois > Chicago metropolitan area

meta_data.Data_summary: A paper-level statement indicating that the data supporting the study are available by requesting them from the authors. This applies to the datasets constructed and used in the workflow but no repository link is provided in the article.
meta_data.Other_Information: Evidence: The data will be made available on request, Location: Extra_info Data availability, Confidence: high
